reports.title: Cognito ATO Chain
Commercetools Client Secret + 369K Discount Codes
reports.description: Production client_secret in frontend JS. Grants access to dpg-prod project with excessive scopes. Enumerated 369,425 discount codes.

reports.title: SSPR Brute Force (1000-keyspace, no rate limiting) + Enrollment Oracle
reports.description: SSPR verification requires only last 3 digits of mobile. No rate limiting, no CAPTCHA, no lockout. 2000+ attempts across 2 SSPR-enrolled accounts with zero blocking. Differential error types reveal enrollment status.

reports.title: Django DEBUG + Brute Force (410 attempts, 0 blocks)
reports.description: Django DEBUG=True exposes all 42 URL patterns. Admin login and daemon API accept unlimited auth attempts. 410 consecutive failures with zero blocking, flat ~450ms timing.

notes.text: ember_bugcrowd asked for 400+ attempt proof - sent 410 attempts
notes.author: machine-a

reports.title: Stored XSS via Guest Cart Billing Address API
reports.description: Guest cart billing address REST API stores raw HTML in firstname/lastname/street/city without sanitization. Frontend validates but API does not. Blind XSS fires when admin views order.

notes.text: Monash explicitly excludes ALL XSS from VDP scope - cannot submit
notes.author: machine-a

reports.title: Open Registration + CVE-2024-27889/27890
reports.description: XNAT instance with open registration and known CVEs.

reports.title: Open Registration + Hardcoded DB/Tomcat Creds in Public Repos
reports.description: GitLab with open registration. Public repos contain hardcoded database and Tomcat credentials.

Laravel Ignition Debug Routes (CVE-2021-3129 Potential RCE)

Open DCR - OAuth Token Theft with Persistent Access
reports.description: Unauthenticated DCR endpoint allows registering OAuth clients with attacker-controlled redirect URIs. Full chain: register app, phish via consent screen, steal tokens + PII.

reports.title: Unauthenticated API Exposes Exact GPS Coords + Member IDs
Plaintext Password in URL via Kasada Bot Detection Redirect
MyTardis IDOR - User PII via Sequential IDs
reports.description: User enumeration and PII access via sequential ID parameters.

reports.title: User Enumeration via emailValidation Endpoint
reports.description: GET /api/user/emailValidation/{email} unauthenticated - returns isAvailableForSignUp, entityType (teacher/parent), isPasswordless, hasMandatorySSO.

reports.title: Teacher Profile IDOR - Read Any Teacher's Data
reports.description: GET /api/teacher/{id} - any authenticated teacher can read any other teacher's profile (name, country, schools, join date, admin status). Write blocked (403).

tasks.text: Read shared/URGENT-READ.md and shared/lessons-learned.md (strategic section). Apply new rules to all active targets.
Update status.json (stale since Mar 3). Review all submitted findings against new 'impact first' rules.
Apply two-account IDOR testing to DPG Media, Indeed, REA targets.
Deep ClassDojo IDOR testing - cross-boundary access between teacher A and B, parent IDOR, message threads.
Read disclosed ClassDojo reports on Bugcrowd before testing.

lessons: Every report failed. ~24 reports across 8+ programs, 0 accepted. Optimized for quantity of surface-level findings instead of depth of exploitation.
PROVE exploitation end-to-end. 'I found creds' is not enough - test them against live systems.
Check if behavior is by-design. Read upstream source code/docs before assuming misconfiguration.
CORS without credentials = P5 every time. Same for config exposure, info disclosure without chaining.
Rate conservatively. Submit as P3, let triager upgrade. A P5 hurts reputation.
Bugcrowd brute force standard = 400+ attempts then successful login.
What actually pays: Auth bypass with demo, IDOR with real data access, RCE/SSRF with proof, business logic.
Depth over breadth. Max 2 targets at a time.
Do NOT use em dashes in reports. Use regular hyphens or rewrite.
Monash excludes ALL XSS from VDP scope.

timeline.event: SSPR report SUBMITTED to Monash VDP (P2)
SSPR breakthrough: differential error types found. Brute-forced 2000+ combos.
ClassDojo recon complete. 60+ endpoints, test accounts, initial IDOR findings.
410-attempt brute force proof completed on xnat-dev. Submitted to ember_bugcrowd.
First constructive triager response! ember_bugcrowd engaged instead of P5.
Confirmed ALL Bugcrowd submissions rejected. Root cause: no exploitation proof.
Deep dive on identity.monash.edu. Full API map extracted from JS bundle.
XNAT report submitted to Monash VDP. Optus Report 16 partially sent.
shop.monash.edu deep testing. Stored XSS, admin brute force, email enumeration.
Monash eRC targets: gitlab, XNAT, connect.monash.edu.
Optus deep testing. All blocked by Akamai/WAF.
Initial recon wave. Optus, Canva, MoonPay, SGB, BTCMarkets.
